name: Guadiana Natural Trail and Daimiel Loop
name_original: Daimiel a Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel – circular por Parque Navaseca
description: Explore the Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel on this moderate 22.8-mile mountain biking loop along the Guadiana Natural Trail.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Daimiel offers diverse terrain within the La Mancha plain, characterized by its unique wetland ecosystem. The region's primary natural feature is the Tablas de Daimiel National Park, where the Guadiana and Gigüela rivers converge, creating a distinct habitat. This flat landscape, interspersed with the wetland environment, provides a mix of trails suitable for various skill levels. Riders can expect routes that traverse open plains and skirt the edges of the national park, offering opportunities to observe diverse flora and fauna. Best mountain bike trails around Daimiel The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta de Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel desde Daimiel, a 34.8 miles (56.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Tablas de Daimiel National Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Guadiana Natural Trail and Daimiel Loop, a moderate 22.7 miles (36.6 km) path. This trail follows sections of the Guadiana Natural Trail, providing a mix of natural scenery and open plains. Local mountain bikers also love the Turnoff to El Mirador de la Mancha – Tourist Office loop from Daimiel, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through the La Mancha plain, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Mountain biking around Daimiel is defined by flat plains, wetland ecosystems, and river areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging paths with moderate elevation gains. The routes in Daimiel are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from 18 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Daimiel's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Daimiel?
A: Mountain biking around Daimiel primarily features the flat plains of La Mancha, interspersed with a unique wetland ecosystem, particularly around the Tablas de Daimiel National Park. You'll find a mix of fast, flowing trails across open plains and routes that skirt the edges of the national park, offering diverse scenery.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Daimiel?
A: Yes, Daimiel offers a range of trails for different skill levels. While many routes are moderate, there are &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/guide/1549112/mtb-trails-around-daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;8 easy mountain bike trails&lt;/a&gt; available, which are generally suitable for beginners or families looking for a less challenging ride. These routes typically have minimal elevation gain.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or natural features I can see while mountain biking in Daimiel?
A: The primary natural attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tablas de Daimiel National Park&lt;/a&gt;, a rare wetland ecosystem where the Guadiana and Gigüela rivers converge. Many routes offer views of this unique habitat, which is a haven for diverse bird species. You might also encounter other natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3495956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Hervideros&lt;/a&gt; or various observation points along the trails.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Daimiel?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Daimiel are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027352405/guadiana-natural-trail-and-daimiel-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail and Daimiel Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33717778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turnoff to El Mirador de la Mancha – Tourist Office loop from Daimiel&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Daimiel?
A: The area is particularly popular for cycling during September and October due to more pleasant temperatures. However, spring also offers good conditions with blooming flora. Summers can be very hot, and winters might be cooler, so checking the weather forecast is always recommended.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Daimiel?
A: The mountain bike trails in Daimiel are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from 18 reviews. Riders often praise the unique wetland scenery, the flat plains offering fast rides, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: While many routes are moderate, some trails offer longer distances and more elevation gain for experienced riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31430815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Sierra – Zacatena Cheese Dairy (Daimiel) loop from Daimiel&lt;/a&gt; covers over 63 km with more significant elevation changes, providing a more demanding ride.

Q: Can I find viewpoints along the mountain bike trails in Daimiel?
A: Yes, several routes offer opportunities to enjoy scenic viewpoints, especially those bordering the Tablas de Daimiel National Park. You can find specific &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6285603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;observation points&lt;/a&gt; within the park that provide excellent views of the wetlands and its wildlife.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, you can explore historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38214218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mancha Viewpoint – Navaseca Lagoon loop from Daimiel&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers a mix of natural and cultural sights. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2661834" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatrava la Vieja Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; and various castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Doña Berenguela&lt;/a&gt; are located in the broader region, offering interesting detours.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of mountain bike trails in Daimiel?
A: The trails vary significantly in length and duration. You can find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027464103/vuelta-de-parque-nacional-de-las-tablas-de-daimiel-desde-daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta de Parque Nacional de las Tablas de Daimiel desde Daimiel&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 56 km and takes around 3 hours 37 minutes, to shorter options. The guide features over 50 mountain bike tours, so there's a wide range to choose from based on your available time and fitness.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain bike trails in Daimiel?
A: While specific public transport links directly to trailheads might be limited, Daimiel itself is accessible by regional transport. It's advisable to check local bus or train schedules to Daimiel and plan your route from there, as many trails start directly from the town or its immediate surroundings.

Q: Are there any permits required to mountain bike in the Tablas de Daimiel National Park area?
A: The Tablas de Daimiel National Park is a protected area. While general access to designated trails is usually permitted, it's always best to consult the official park regulations for any specific permits or restrictions, especially if you plan to deviate from marked paths. You can find more information on the park's official website or through resources like &lt;a href="https://www.visitacity.com/en/tablas-de-daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit a City&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tablas_de_Daimiel_National_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedia&lt;/a&gt; for general park information.
tips: About 2 kilometers from Daimiel, between the city and the Guadiana river following the road to El Nuevo, the Navaseca lagoon is part of the set of lagoons in the Daimiel district that were already described in the 16th century in the topographical reports ordered by Philip II.
Great area next to the Tablas de Daimiel National Park.

Its construction began in the year 1738, when the daimieleño priest Don Dionisio de Montealegre obtained authorization from the Archbishop of Toledo, Don Luis de Borbón, to build a sanctuary and through alms and offerings enough money was collected for the start of the works. .

In the 16th century, and due to population growth, the Daimiel authorities asked Emperor Carlos I for a license to build another parish church. The monarch agreed and the church was finished in 1587.

The church has a Latin cross floor plan, of the Doric order, brick vaults, stone arches and pottery tiled floors (today covered by a wooden platform).

The original altarpiece was golden, in the Churrigueresque style, and was destroyed during the Spanish Civil War.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This moderate mountain bike route is approximately 36.6 km long and generally takes around 2 hours and 30 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this route is suitable for riders with a good level of fitness and some mountain biking experience. While it has a relatively low elevation gain of about 119 meters, the varied terrain might be challenging for absolute beginners. More advanced riders will find it an enjoyable and scenic ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Guadiana Natural Trail and Daimiel Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, offering a mix of surfaces typical of the Guadiana Natural Trail. You'll encounter wide dirt tracks, some sections that might follow old railway lines, and potentially some areas with Mediterranean scrubland. The route is generally flat with gentle slopes, making for a pleasant ride through diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll pass by the serene Navaseca Lagoon and ride through a peaceful Reforested pine forest. Additionally, you can spot historical religious buildings like the Cristo de la Luz Hermitage and the Church of San Pedro Apóstol (Daimiel).

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures and occasional rain.

faqs.question: Is the Guadiana Natural Trail and Daimiel Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near wildlife, or within protected natural spaces like the vicinity of Tablas de Daimiel National Park. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your dog.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Given that the route starts and ends in Daimiel and includes the Tablas de Daimiel area, you can typically find parking within Daimiel town or at designated parking areas near the entrance to Tablas de Daimiel National Park. Always check local signage for specific parking regulations and availability.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking near Tablas de Daimiel National Park?
faqs.answer: While the route passes near and through areas associated with Tablas de Daimiel National Park, mountain biking on the general trails like the Guadiana Natural Trail sections typically does not require a specific permit or entrance fee. However, access to certain restricted areas within the National Park itself might have specific regulations or require prior authorization. It's always best to check the official park website for the most current information regarding access and regulations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant sections of the official Camino Natural del Guadiana (30%) and the Camino de Molemocho a Puente Navarro / GR114 CN Guadiana (19%). You'll also ride along parts of the camino de Puente Navarro (26%) and the Carril de Griñón al Nuevo / GR114a - Ojos del Guadiana (7%), among other smaller local paths.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on this trail?
faqs.answer: As the route is in the vicinity of the Guadiana River and Tablas de Daimiel National Park, an important wetland, you might encounter a variety of bird species, especially waterfowl. The broader Guadiana Valley Natural Park is also a sanctuary for endangered species, including the Iberian Lynx, though sightings are rare. Keep an eye out for local flora and fauna, particularly in the more natural sections.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the trail, or should I carry my own?
faqs.answer: It is highly recommended to carry sufficient water for your entire ride. While you might pass through Daimiel where you can resupply, there are generally no dedicated water sources directly on the trail sections, especially in the more rural areas. Always be prepared, particularly during warmer months.
